subject: Chemistry
solution:
Meta–chlorobenzaldehyde will undergo Cannizzaro reaction with 50% KOH to give mchlorobenzoate ion and m-chlorobenzyl alcohol. 

Since m-chlorobenzaldehyde does not contain α hydrogen thus with 50% KOH it undergoes the Cannizzaro reaction. The Cannizzaro reaction for the m-chlorobenzaldehyde is given as:

Since it is a disproportionation reaction so one molecule of m-chlorobenzaldehyde is reduced to m-chlorobenzyl alcohol at the cost of others which is oxidized potassium m-chlorobenzoate.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Matching reagents with aldehyde reaction types:

A (H2, Pd-BaSO4) is the Lindlar/Rosenmund catalyst used in Rosenmund Reduction to convert acyl chlorides to aldehydes, matching with II.

B (SnCl2, HCl) is used in Stephen Reaction where nitriles are converted to aldehydes via imine intermediate, matching with IV.

C (CrO2Cl2, CS2) is Jones-like reagent used in Etard Reaction for oxidizing methyl groups on aromatic rings to aldehydes, matching with I.

D (CO, HCl, Anhyd. AlCl3) is the Gatterman-Koch Reaction which directly converts benzene to benzaldehyde using CO and AlCl3 catalyst, matching with III.

Therefore the correct matching is A-II, B-IV, C-I, D-III

solution: Markovnikov's addition states that, in hydrohalogenation of an unsymmetrical alkene, the hydrogen atom in the hydrogen halide forms a bond with the doubly bonded carbon atom in the alkene, bearing the greater number of hydrogen atoms.

solution:
In Fehling’s solution, the reaction between copper(II) ions and aliphatic aldehyde is represented as;

RCHO+2Cu2++5OH-→RCOO-+Cu2O+3H2O

Hence ,Acetaldehyde CH3CHO, Methanal HCHO, and cyclohexane carbaldehyde 

compounds  gives positive Fehling's test.

Hence, the answer is 3.

subject: Chemistry

solution:
DIBAL-H i.e., Di isobutyl aluminium hydride causes partial reduction of cyanide group along with the ester group and further hydrolysis forms corresponding aldehyde as the product. The reaction is as follows,

CH3CH25COOC2H5→ DIBAL- H,H2OCH3CH25CHO

The Clemmensen reduction is a reaction that is used to reduce aldehydes or ketones to alkanes using hydrochloric acid and zinc amalgam.

C6H5COC6H5→Zn(Hg)& conc. HClC6H5CH2C6H5

A Grignard reagent is an organomagnesium compound which can be described by the chemical formula ‘R-Mg-X’ . Here aldehyde will react with Grignard's reagent to give alcohol.

C6H5CHO→H2OCH3MgBrC6H5CH(OH)CH3

CH3COCH2COOC2H5→NaBH4,H+CH3CH(OH)CH2COOC2H5

In this reaction, NaBH4,H+ is used as a reducing agent, to get CH3CH(OH)CH2COOC2H5 as a product.

Hence option B is the answer.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Ozonolysis with O3 converts alkenes into carbonyl compounds.

Alcohol upon reaction with protic acids tends to lose a molecule of water to form alkenes. These reactions are known as dehydrogenation or dehydration of alcohols. It is an example of an elimination reaction.The order of the ease of dehydration of alcohols is: tertiary > secondary > primary.

subject: Chemistry
Chemistry
solution:
(A) Aldehydes give red precipitate with Fehling's solution.

(B) Phenols give violet colour with neutral ferric chloride solution

(C) Primary amines give isocyanides with chloroform in the presence of alkali. This reaction known as carbylamine reaction.

(D) 1-phenylethanol give haloform reaction with sodium hypochlorite. The products are benzoate and chloroform.

subject: Chemistry
solution: Formation of an acetal occurs when the hydroxyl group of a hemiacetal becomes protonated and is lost as water. The carbocation that is produced is then rapidly attacked by a molecule of alcohol. Loss of the proton from the attached alcohol gives the acetal.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Diisobutylaluminium hydride, AlH(i-Bu)2  also known as DIBAL or DIBAL-H, is an exceedingly useful and versatile reducing agent. It is an electrophilic reducing agent, usually employed in selective reductions of esters or nitriles to aldehydes

So, in first step nitrile group will be reduced to aldehyde group. 

In second step cross aldol condensation reaction will take place which will give mixture of products. Out of which one of the product will be option B.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Diisobutylaluminum hydride (DIBAL-H) is an organoaluminum reagent typically used for the reduction esters or nitriles to aldehydes. 

In the second step intramolecular aldol condensation reaction takes place. Cyclization occurs if the α carbon atom and the second carbonyl carbon atom can bond to form a five- or six-membered ring.

The Clemmensen reduction is a reaction that is used to reduce aldehydes or ketones to alkanes using hydrochloric acid and zinc amalgam. In the same condition, alcohol converts to alkenes.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
RCHO+2AgNH32OH→RCOONH4+2Ag+3NH3+H2O

The RCHO is cyclohexane carbaldehyde (C6H11CHO). The molar mass is112g.

The number of moles of cyclohexane carbaldehyde=131.8×103112=1.176×103 mol

1 mole of aldehyde produces 3 moles of NH3

1.176×103 mol aldehyde will produce

=3×1.176×103 moles of NH3

Mass of NH3 produced =3×1.176×103×17

=59.97×103 g

≈ 60 kg
